summary: 2026-06-23 12:45:10 PDT — Re-reviewed `c6662f2...HEAD` and `c6662f2..HEAD` against `reviews/security-program.md`. The diff is limited to docs/review artifacts; no application logic changed. The old CodeQL-only/security-scan wording is gone, but the required-check DRY fix is not complete because dependency-management still restates the merge gate outside SSDLC.

findings.title: Dependency policy still restates merge-gate checks
findings.claim: The DRY decision says `ssdlc.md` § Merge control is the single prose source enumerating required checks, with dependency-management generalized to the stable PR/all-required-status-checks wording. This file still says every update PR goes through the `standard review + test + CodeQL gate before merge`, and later says dependency changes require `passing tests and SAST`, so the volatile check list still exists outside SSDLC and can drift again.
findings.suggestion: Replace those dependency-management sentences with a generic reference to the standard SSDLC merge gate/all required status checks, linking to `ssdlc.md` § Merge control, without naming individual required checks outside SSDLC.
